fields.Text:
Fever, back pain, progressive myelopathy, and MRI disc/vertebral infection. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Spondylodiscitis/discitis-osteomyelitis}}
fields.Extra:
Q1. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Spondylodiscitis also referred to as discitis-osteomyelitis, is characterized by an infection involving the intervertebral disc and adjacent vertebrae. Spread is most commonly hematogenous. MRI is the imaging modality of choice due to its very high sensitivity and specificity.




Open review Q1

fields.Text: Spondylodiscitis/discitis-osteomyelitis most commonly spreads by {{c1::hematogenous seeding}}.
fields.Extra:
Q1. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Spondylodiscitis also referred to as discitis-osteomyelitis, is characterized by an infection involving the intervertebral disc and adjacent vertebrae. Spread is most commonly hematogenous. MRI is the imaging modality of choice due to its very high sensitivity and specificity.





Open review Q1

fields.Text:
Acute RLQ pain that is easing with enlarged edematous ovary and peripheral follicles. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Ovarian torsion-detorsion}}

1/3
fields.Extra:
Q2. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Spontaneous torsion and detorsion can occur before ischemia develops, causing massive ovarian edema that mimics torsion imaging findings (enlarged ovary with peripheral follicles) but lacks the characteristic twisted pedicle sign. This creates ongoing risk for recurrent torsion episodes and requires clinical correlation to distinguish from acute torsion.




Open review Q2

fields.Text: Ovarian torsion-detorsion can cause {{c1::massive ovarian edema with peripheral follicles}} without established ischemia.
fields.Extra:
Q2. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Spontaneous torsion and detorsion can occur before ischemia develops, causing massive ovarian edema that mimics torsion imaging findings (enlarged ovary with peripheral follicles) but lacks the characteristic twisted pedicle sign. This creates ongoing risk for recurrent torsion episodes and requires clinical correlation to distinguish from acute torsion.







Open review Q2

fields.Text:
10 weeks amenorrhea with large empty gestational sac and ovarian functional cysts. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Anembryonic gestation with functional ovarian cysts}}

1/3
fields.Extra:
Q3. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Gestational sac ≥25 mm without embryo at appropriate gestational age represents anembryonic pregnancy—diagnostic of early pregnancy loss. No need for follow-up imaging as this meets definitive criteria for non-viable pregnancy requiring counseling and management planning.




Open review Q3

fields.Text: A gestational sac measuring at least {{c1::25 mm without an embryo}} is diagnostic of anembryonic pregnancy/early pregnancy loss.
fields.Extra:
Q3. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Gestational sac ≥25 mm without embryo at appropriate gestational age represents anembryonic pregnancy—diagnostic of early pregnancy loss. No need for follow-up imaging as this meets definitive criteria for non-viable pregnancy requiring counseling and management planning.







Open review Q3

fields.Text: An absorbed fetal dose of 25 mGy from CT causes {{c1::no significant increased risk of birth defects or intellectual disability}}, with only a small increased lifetime cancer risk.
fields.Extra:
Q4. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Radiation doses below 100 mGy during pregnancy do not cause identifiable developmental defects. Counseling should emphasize positive outcomes, stating the child will have nearly the same chances of being healthy as any other child, with only a very small increase in lifetime cancer risk that is typically negligible.




Open review Q4

fields.Text:
Occult breast primary presenting as abnormal axillary node with negative mammogram/US. Next imaging?

{{c1::Breast MRI}}
fields.Extra:
Q5. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Breast MRI excels at detecting noncalcified occult lesions and problem-solving mammographic asymmetries but has limited utility for excluding malignancy in calcified lesions.




Open review Q5

fields.Text: When axillary metastasis suggests occult breast cancer and mammogram/US do not show a primary, the best next test is {{c1::breast MRI}}.
fields.Extra:
Q5. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Breast MRI excels at detecting noncalcified occult lesions and problem-solving mammographic asymmetries but has limited utility for excluding malignancy in calcified lesions.

{{c1::Liver}}

Q6. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The images demonstrate numerous renal cysts (green arrows) and hepatic cysts (blue arrows).

fields.Text: In autosomal dominant polycystic kidney disease, the most frequently involved extrarenal organ is the {{c1::liver}}.
fields.Extra:
Q6. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The images demonstrate numerous renal cysts (green arrows) and hepatic cysts (blue arrows).

fields.Text: Carotid stenosis severity on Doppler US is most often graded by {{c1::internal carotid artery peak systolic velocity}}.
fields.Extra:
Q7. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Peak systolic velocity is the most important metric for assessing carotid stenosis severity on Doppler ultrasound.

fields.Text: Root cause analysis is a {{c1::structured method for analyzing serious adverse events}}.
fields.Extra:
Q8. Correct; difficulty: easy. Initially developed to analyze industrial accidents, RCA is now widely deployed as an error analysis tool in health care.

{{c1::Autosomal recessive polycystic kidney disease}}

Q10. Correct; difficulty: easy. The image shows echogenic foci (yellow arrows), which represent calcifications within the renal medulla that demonstrate posterior shadowing (blue arrows).

{{c1::Trochlear dysplasia, patella alta, and lateralized tibial tuberosity}}

1/2
fields.Extra:
Q11. Correct; difficulty: easy. The axial fat-saturated T2 image shows a reciprocal marrow edema lateral femoral condyle (red arrow) and the medial patellar facet (orange arrow).




Open review Q11

fields.Text: Patellar instability/dislocation is predisposed by {{c1::trochlear dysplasia, patella alta, and lateralization of the tibial tuberosity}}.
fields.Extra:
Q11. Correct; difficulty: easy. The axial fat-saturated T2 image shows a reciprocal marrow edema lateral femoral condyle (red arrow) and the medial patellar facet (orange arrow).

fields.Text: Increasing the PET ROI size around a lesion can decrease average SUV by {{c1::volume averaging and increased statistical variability}}.
fields.Extra:
Q12. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: To measure SUV, a 2D or 3D region of interest (ROI) is centered upon a target. By increasing the ROI there is more volume averaging and more statistical variability, leading to an overall decrease in SUV.




Open review Q12

fields.Text: MRI Zone I is the {{c1::unrestricted public access}} zone.
fields.Extra:
Q13. Correct; difficulty: easy. There are several safety measures involved in the use of magnetic resonance (MR) scanners, primarily due to the scanners’ strong magnetic fields.




Open review Q13

fields.Text:
Anterior neck pain, thyrotoxicosis, and absent thyroid uptake. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Subacute thyroiditis}}

1/2
fields.Extra:
Q14. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: The combination of thyrotoxic symptoms with anterior neck pain and absent thyroid uptake is most compatible with subacute thyroiditis, representing destructive thyroid inflammation that releases preformed hormones while simultaneously damaging the iodine-trapping mechanism. Supportive care rather than antithyroid drugs is usually indicated, as the thyrotoxicosis is self-limited and will typically progress through hyperthyroid, hypothyroid, and recovery phases over several months.




Open review Q14

fields.Text: Subacute thyroiditis causes painful thyrotoxicosis with {{c1::markedly decreased/absent radioiodine uptake}}.
fields.Extra:
Q14. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: The combination of thyrotoxic symptoms with anterior neck pain and absent thyroid uptake is most compatible with subacute thyroiditis, representing destructive thyroid inflammation that releases preformed hormones while simultaneously damaging the iodine-trapping mechanism. Supportive care rather than antithyroid drugs is usually indicated, as the thyrotoxicosis is self-limited and will typically progress through hyperthyroid, hypothyroid, and recovery phases over several months.






Open review Q14

fields.Text: The minimum single acute dose threshold for lens opacification is about {{c1::0.5 Gy}}.
fields.Extra:
Q15. Correct; difficulty: hard. There are several values related to radiation exposure laid out in the annual RISC study guide that are highly testable and worth knowing.




Open review Q15

fields.Text:
RUQ pain with nonvisualized gallbladder on HIDA. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Acute cholecystitis}}
fields.Extra:
Q16. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Hepatobiliary scintigraphy is ordered when ultrasound is equivocal or clinical suspicion for acute cholecystitis remains high despite negative ultrasound. In contrast to ultrasound, it can provide functional assessment of cystic duct patency. Nonvisualization of gallbladder with normal liver function is highly sensitive for acute cholecystitis.




Open review Q16

fields.Text: On HIDA, nonvisualization of the gallbladder with normal hepatic uptake/excretion supports {{c1::acute cholecystitis}}.
fields.Extra:
Q16. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Hepatobiliary scintigraphy is ordered when ultrasound is equivocal or clinical suspicion for acute cholecystitis remains high despite negative ultrasound. In contrast to ultrasound, it can provide functional assessment of cystic duct patency. Nonvisualization of gallbladder with normal liver function is highly sensitive for acute cholecystitis.





Open review Q16

fields.Text:
Non-inverted angiogram with negative contrast in renal failure. Contrast agent?

{{c1::Carbon dioxide}}

1/2
fields.Extra:
Q17. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Carbon dioxide is appropriate for DSA in patients with chronic kidney disease or severe iodine allergy. Carbon dioxide allows intervention while significantly reducing risk of contrast-induced nephropathy compared to iodinated contrast.




Open review Q17

fields.Text: Carbon dioxide angiography is useful in patients with {{c1::chronic kidney disease or severe iodinated contrast allergy}}.
fields.Extra:

{{c1::Marchiafava-Bignami disease}}

fields.Text:
IPMN with enhancing mural nodule. High-risk resection feature?

{{c1::Mural nodule greater than 5 mm}}

1/2
fields.Extra:
Q20. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: According to the Kyoto guidelines, intraductal papillary neoplasm (IPMN) should undergo resection if any high-risk features are present. These high-risk features are obstructive jaundice with cystic lesion in the head of the pancreas, solid component or mural nodule >5 mm, main pancreatic duct >10 mm, or suspicious cytology results.

fields.Text:
Supine chest radiograph with abnormally lucent deep lateral costophrenic sulcus. Sign?

{{c1::Deep sulcus sign}}
fields.Extra:
Q25. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: The deep sulcus sign indicates pneumothorax on supine chest radiographs, appearing as increased lucency with an inferiorly displaced lateral costal margin due to air accumulating in the lateral subpulmonic pleural recess. This subtle finding is important since pneumothoraces are often inapparent on supine radiographs.




Open review Q25

fields.Text: The deep sulcus sign indicates {{c1::pneumothorax on a supine chest radiograph}}.
fields.Extra:
Q25. Correct; difficulty: hard. This subtle finding is important since pneumothoraces are often inapparent on supine radiographs.

fields.Text: A mistake is a {{c1::planning/judgment failure}}, whereas slips/lapses reflect attentional or execution failures.
fields.Extra:
Q26. Correct; difficulty: moderate. In some contexts, errors are dichotomized as slips or mistakes, based on the cognitive psychology of task-oriented behavior.




Open review Q26

fields.Text:
Post-TACE patient with hepatic abscess risk anatomy. Key risk factor?

{{c1::Prior hepatic bilioenterostomy}}

1/4
fields.Extra:
Q27. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Altered biliary anatomy dramatically increases hepatic abscess risk post-TACE. Primary risk factors include bilioenteric anastomosis and incompetent sphincter of Oddi from stenting or sphincterotomy. These create communication between biliary tree and enable biliary colonization by enteric flora, predisposing to ascending infection.




Open review Q27

fields.Text: Prior bilioenteric anastomosis or incompetent sphincter of Oddi markedly increases {{c1::hepatic abscess risk after TACE}}.
fields.Extra:
Q27. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Altered biliary anatomy dramatically increases hepatic abscess risk post-TACE. These create communication between biliary tree and enable biliary colonization by enteric flora, predisposing to ascending infection.








Open review Q27

fields.Text: Expected mammographic changes after lumpectomy/radiation include {{c1::skin thickening/edema, increased density, scar, fat necrosis, and dystrophic calcifications}}.
fields.Extra:
Q28. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: The expected changes on mammography after breast conservative surgery and radiation include skin thickening or edema, parenchymal edema, post-operative fluid collection, scar, fat necrosis, and dystrophic calcifications. Skin thickening/edema can be up to 1 cm in thickness and is most pronounced in the first 6 months after treatment.

fields.Text:
Cyst-like renal lesion that communicates with the collecting system on delayed imaging. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Calyceal diverticulum}}

1/4
fields.Extra:
Q30. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Calyceal diverticulum appears as a cyst-like outpouching on CT that may initially mimic a simple renal cyst. CT urography with contrast enhancement and delayed phases can demonstrate communication with the collecting system, distinguishing it from other cystic lesions.

{{c1::Left renal infarction}}

fields.Text: Decreasing receiver bandwidth can increase MRI acquisition time by {{c1::slowing signal readout/lengthening echo spacing}}.
fields.Extra:
Q33. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Decreasing bandwidth slows signal readout, lengthening echo spacing and increasing scan time. However, lower bandwidth improves signal-to-noise ratio by reducing noise and allowing more signal averaging during the longer readout period. This creates a fundamental trade-off between scan speed and image quality in MRI acquisition.




Open review Q33

fields.Text:
CT with gastric pneumatosis and hepatic abnormality. Most likely explanation?

{{c1::Gastric wall ischemia}}

1/6
fields.Extra:
Q34. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Gastric pneumatosis appears as intramural air pockets within the gastric wall on CT without wall thickening or inflammatory changes. This finding indicates mucosal barrier disruption from ischemia or other non-infectious causes. It must be differentiated from emphysematous gastritis, which shows wall thickening and systemic toxicity.

fields.Text: Gastric pneumatosis without inflammatory wall thickening can reflect {{c1::gastric wall ischemia or mucosal barrier disruption}}.

{{c1::Pulmonary abscess}}
fields.Extra:

fields.Text: Pulmonary abscesses most commonly develop from {{c1::aspiration pneumonia with anaerobic/polymicrobial infection}}.

Breast hamartoma/fibroadenolipoma appearance. BI-RADS category?

{{c1::BI-RADS 2}}

fields.Extra:
Q39. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Breast hamartomas, also known as fibroadenolipomas, are benign breast lesions characterized by a "breast within a breast" appearance on mammogram. They appear as a well-circumscribed round or oval mass surrounded by a thin capsule on mammogram and composed of both fat and soft-tissue internal densities.

fields.Extra:
Q44. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Adamantinomatous craniopharyngioma usually presents as a solid/cystic sellar mass with calcification and is most common in children.

fields.Text:
Hematospermia with echogenic shadowing material in seminal vesicles. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Seminal vesicle calculi}}

1/2
fields.Extra:
Q45. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Seminal vesicle calcifications appear as echogenic material with posterior acoustic shadowing on transrectal ultrasound and can be a cause of hematospermia. Associations include diabetes, hyperparathyroidism and chronic infection/inflammation.

fields.Text: Three-point Dixon MRI reduces {{c1::field inhomogeneity errors}} compared with two-point Dixon.
fields.Extra:
Q46. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Different implementations of the Dixon technique have been used to quantify fat and water in various parts of the human body. The advantage of the 3-point Dixon technique is decreased field inhomogeneity errors.

fields.Text:
Chest CTA with focal coronary dilation. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Coronary artery aneurysm}}
fields.Extra:
Q47. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Coronary artery aneurysms are defined as ≥50% diameter increase. The most commonly etiologies are atherosclerotic (in the US) and Kawasaki disease (worldwide). Size >8mm indicates higher thrombotic risk, while hemopericardium suggests rupture requiring emergent intervention.




Open review Q47

fields.Text: A coronary artery aneurysm is defined as {{c1::at least 50% focal diameter enlargement}}.
fields.Extra:

Size >8mm indicates higher thrombotic risk, while hemopericardium suggests rupture requiring emergent intervention.

fields.Text:
Breast implant pain with extracapsular echogenic snowstorm silicone. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Extracapsular silicone implant rupture}}

1/4
fields.Extra:
Q51. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Extracapsular silicone implant rupture appears on mammography as dense silicone material in breast tissue outside the fibrous capsule, and on ultrasound shows the characteristic "snowstorm" appearance with dense shadowing caused by silicone deposition in breast parenchyma, lymph nodes, or systemic organs.

fields.Text:
Painful scrotal swelling with hyperemic epididymis/testis. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Epididymo-orchitis}}

1/2
fields.Extra:
Q54. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Epididymo-orchitis shows enlarged epididymis and/or testis with variable echogenicity. The key finding is increased blood flow on color Doppler. Associated findings include reactive hydrocele and scrotal wall thickening. Complications include abscess formation and post-inflammatory ischemia.




Open review Q54

fields.Text: Epididymo-orchitis is suggested by {{c1::increased Doppler flow in an enlarged epididymis/testis}}.
